Finance Committee Meeting with exhibit book containing BudgetWatch January 2021 Flash Report, Finance 2020 Year End Special Report, and DRAFT MTA 3rd Quarter Consolidated Financial Statements

tool_args.label: CARES Act Full Receipt
tool_args.raw_text: The full complement of $4.009 billion in aid from the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security (CARES) Act has been received and distributed to Agencies based on net operating losses reported through July.

payload.claim_text: The full complement of $4.009 billion in aid from the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security (CARES) Act has been received and distributed to Agencies based on net operating losses reported through July.
payload.statement: The MTA received the full $4.009 billion in CARES Act aid and distributed to Agencies.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Debt Service Expense 2020
tool_args.raw_text: Debt Service expense for 2020 was $2,703.0 million, which were $31.3 million or 1.1% favorable due to lower than budgeted variable rates and timing related to the non-recording of a RAN debt service payment.

payload.claim_text: Debt Service expense for 2020 was $2,703.0 million, which were $31.3 million or 1.1% favorable due to lower than budgeted variable rates and timing related to the non-recording of a RAN debt service payment.
payload.statement: 2020 debt service was $2,703.0 million, $31.3 million favorable to forecast.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Peak subway ridership decline April 2020
tool_args.raw_text: At height of pandemic, subway and commuter railroad ridership dropped by approximately 95% as compared to similar timeframe in 2019

payload.raw_value_text: At height of pandemic, subway and commuter railroad ridership dropped by approximately 95% as compared to similar timeframe in 2019

tool_args.label: PMT BANs Issuance to MLF Dec 2020
tool_args.raw_text: On December 17, 2020, MTA completed a direct placement of $2,907,280,000 of Payroll Mobility Tax Bond Anticipation Notes (BANs), Series 2020A to the MLF, which have an interest rate of 1.33% and mature on December 15, 2023

payload.claim_text: On December 17, 2020, MTA completed a direct placement of $2,907,280,000 of Payroll Mobility Tax Bond Anticipation Notes (BANs), Series 2020A to the MLF, which have an interest rate of 1.33% and mature on December 15, 2023
payload.statement: MTA placed $2,907,280,000 of PMT BANs with MLF at 1.33% interest maturing Dec 15, 2023.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: McKinsey Revenue Loss Analysis
tool_args.raw_text: McKinsey's COVID-19 Analysis Report, published May 1, indicated approximately $10.3 billion revenue loss for the 2-year total from 2020 and 2021

payload.claim_text: McKinsey's COVID-19 Analysis Report, published May 1, indicated approximately $10.3 billion revenue loss for the 2-year total from 2020 and 2021
payload.statement: McKinsey estimated $10.3 billion revenue loss for 2020-2021.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: McKinsey Ridership Recovery Rates
tool_args.raw_text: Preliminary results of updated McKinsey analysis, as incorporated in the December Financial Plan, are more pessimistic than May 1 analysis, indicating ridership recovery rates of 27% for 2021, 54% for 2022, 71% for 2023, and 79% for 2024

payload.raw_value_text: ridership recovery rates of 27% for 2021, 54% for 2022, 71% for 2023, and 79% for 2024
payload.description: Preliminary results of updated McKinsey analysis indicating projected ridership recovery rates

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Farebox Revenue YTD Dec 2020
NYCT Subway Ridership Decline 9mo 2020 vs 2019
tool_args.raw_text: MTA New York City Transit subway ridership declined by 757.5 million trips (60.0%)

payload.raw_value_text: NYCT subway ridership declined by 757.5 million trips (60.0%)

tool_args.label: NYCT Bus Ridership Decline 9mo 2020 vs 2019
tool_args.raw_text: MTA New York City Transit bus declined by 277.6 million trips (66.4%)

payload.raw_value_text: NYCT bus declined by 277.6 million trips (66.4%)

tool_args.label: B&T Traffic Decline 9mo 2020 vs 2019
tool_args.raw_text: Vehicle traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels facilities through the third quarter decreased by 62.1 million crossings (25.1%) compared with 2019 levels

payload.raw_value_text: Vehicle traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels facilities through the third quarter decreased by 62.1 million crossings (25.1%)

tool_args.label: Municipal Market Turbulence 2020
tool_args.raw_text: The municipal market was very turbulent during 2020 In mid-March, the municipal market essentially shut down and only small transactions were priced and most new issues scheduled to price were pulled. 30-year MMD increased by nearly 200 bps to 3.37% over a two-week period

payload.claim_text: The municipal market was very turbulent during 2020 In mid-March, the municipal market essentially shut down and only small transactions were priced and most new issues scheduled to price were pulled. 30-year MMD increased by nearly 200 bps to 3.37% over a two-week period
payload.statement: Municipal market shut down in mid-March 2020; 30-year MMD spiked nearly 200 bps to 3.37%

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: TRB Credit Downgraded 7 Times 2020
tool_args.raw_text: MTA's Transportation Revenue Bond credit was downgraded seven times in 2020

payload.claim_text: MTA's Transportation Revenue Bond credit was downgraded seven times in 2020
payload.statement: MTA's TRB credit was downgraded 7 times in 2020 by S&P, Fitch, Moody's, and KBRA.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: MTA COVID-19 Projected Deficits July Plan 2020-2024
tool_args.raw_text: MTA projected total deficits attributable to COVID-19 pandemic impacts in the MTA July Plan (calendar years 2020 through 2024) of approximately $16.2 billion

payload.description: Total projected deficits attributable to COVID-19 pandemic impacts

tool_args.label: CARES Act $4 Billion Federal Relief
tool_args.raw_text: In March 2020, the CARES Act was enacted, providing $4.0 billion in federal relief for operating costs

tool_args.label: Second COVID Relief Bill $4 Billion Dec 2020
tool_args.raw_text: In December 2020, the second COVID Relief Bill was enacted, providing another $4.0 billion in federal relief for operating costs in 2021

payload.raw_value_text: another $4.0 billion in federal relief for operating costs in 2021
